_comment: WC2026 Group L. Standings before this match: England 4 pts, Ghana 4, Croatia 0 (lost 2-4 to England), Panama 0 (lost 0-1 to Ghana). Both Croatia and Panama have played once and sit on 0 -> a near-must-win six-pointer; the loser is virtually eliminated. Croatia the quality side (experienced, technical) but leaked 4 to England; Panama organized and low-scoring (0-1 Ghana). Both NEED the win -> likely an open-ish game (not a one-sided park-the-bus). Cards not modelled (referee TBD). CAUTION (England-Ghana lesson): Panama is organized, so a tight low-scoring game / draw is a live tail; don't over-lean on a Croatia rout.

meta.stakes: Both on 0 points after MD1 (Croatia 2-4 England; Panama 0-1 Ghana), with England and Ghana on 4: a near-must-win for both, the loser virtually eliminated. Croatia the favourite on quality; Panama an organized, limited underdog.

home.name: Croatia
notes: Experienced, technical European side; scored 2 vs England. attack 1.22.
Leaked 4 to England (open game), but Panama is far less threatening -> defense 0.95.

Calibrated in line with the market (~59%): an initial lambda of 1.85 put Croatia ~3.6 pts ABOVE market; trimmed to respect the England-Ghana lesson (organized underdog, don't overrate the favourite). Both must win -> open-ish, rho -0.06.

away.name: Panama
notes: Organized CONCACAF side; lost 0-1 to Ghana, blunt going forward. attack 0.74.
Disciplined but not elite vs Croatia's quality -> defense 1.0.
Must win too -> will have to come out, which opens the game (less of a low-block trap than England-Ghana).

_postmatch.result: Croatia 1-0 Panama. Tight, low-scoring win; Croatia clean sheet, BTTS No, 1 goal total.
_postmatch.verdict: Clean hit. The favorite won by the model's #1 scoreline (1-0, 13.2%). Every read landed: Under 2.5 (53.9%), BTTS No, Croatia clean sheet (44.9%), low total (xG 2.52 -> 1 goal). The trim-to-market was right: I cut Croatia from an initial 62% down to 58.6% (organized Panama, England-Ghana lesson) and called a tense 1-0 -- exactly what happened.
_postmatch.lesson: Second straight 1-0 hit (with Colombia 1-0 DR Congo) where the proven-organized-underdog read produced a tight, low-scoring favourite win. Trimming an over-confident initial lambda toward the market keeps the draw/Under correctly live; don't inflate a modest favourite vs a disciplined side.
